briefdescription.para: Continue.
OK.
Success.
Created.
Accepted.
Non-Authoritative Information
No Content.
Reset Content.
Partial Content.
Multiple Choices.
Moved Permanently.
Moved temporarily.
See Other.
Not modified.
Use Proxy.
Bad Request - server couldn’t understand request.
Unauthorized.
Payment Required.
Forbidden - operation is understood but refused.
Not Found.
Method Not Allowed.
Not Acceptable.
Proxy Authentication Required.
Request Time Out.
Conflict.
Gone.
Length Required.
Precondition Failed.
Requested Entity Too Large.
Requested URL Too Large.
Unsupported media type.
Internal serve Error.
Not Implemented.
Bad Gateway.
Service Unavailable.
Gateway Timeout.
HTTP Version not supported.
Database Full.
Database Locked.
OBEX Response Code.
Number of objects (used by Connect)
Name of the object (often a file name)
Type of object - e.g.

detaileddescription.para: text, html, binary, manufacturer specific

briefdescription.para: The length of the object in bytes.
Date/time stamp – ISO 8601 version - preferred.
Date/time stamp – 4 byte version (for compatibility only)
Text description of the object.
Name of service that operation is targeted to.
An HTTP 1.x header.
A chunk of the object body.
The final chunk of the object body.
Identifies the OBEX application, used to tell if talking to a peer.
An identifier used for OBEX connection multiplexing.
Extended application request & response information.
Authentication digest-challenge.
Authentication digest-response.
Indicates the creator of an object.
Uniquely identifies the network client (OBEX server).
OBEX Object class of object.
Parameters used in session commands/responses.
Sequence number used in each OBEX packet for reliability.
Specifies the action to be performed (used in ACTION operation).
The destination object name (used in certain ACTION operations).
4-byte bit mask for setting permissions.
1-byte value to setup Single Response Mode (SRM).
1-byte value for setting parameters used during Single Response Mode (SRM).
OBEX Header ID.
OBEX disconnected.
OBEX in connecting state.
OBEX ready for upper layer traffic on it.
OBEX in disconnecting state.
Life-span states of OBEX.

detaileddescription.para: Used only by internal APIs dealing with setting OBEX to proper state depending on operational context.
OBEX enters the BT_OBEX_CONNECTING state upon bt_obex_connect, or upon returning from bt_obex_server_ops::connect.
When OBEX leaves the BT_OBEX_CONNECTING state and enters the BT_OBEX_CONNECTED, bt_obex_connect_rsp or bt_obex_client_ops::connect is called with the response code BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS.

When OBEX enters the BT_OBEX_DISCONNECTED from other states, bt_obex_client_ops::disconnect or bt_obex_connect_rsp is called with the response code BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS. Or OBEX transport enters the disconnected state from other OBEX transport states.

briefdescription.para: Backup a level before applying name (equivalent to ../ on many systems).
Don't create folder if it does not exist, return an error instead.
OBEX SetPath operation flags.
Copy Object Action.
Move/Rename Object Action.
Set Object Permissions Action.
OBEX Actions.
Converts a OBEX response code to string.

simplesect.para: The string representation of the response code. If CONFIG_BT_OEBX_RSP_CODE_TO_STR is not enabled, this just returns the empty string.

briefdescription.para: OBEX server register.

detaileddescription.para: Register a OBEX server with the specific UUID.

The OBEX object of the server should be set before calling the function. If the UUID is NULL, any connection request will be accepted. Or, the space of the UUID should be retained. Before calling the API, bt_obex_server::ops should be initialized with valid address of type bt_obex_server_ops object. And the bt_obex_server::obex should be initialized with the valid OBEX object of type IrDA Oject Exchange Protocol (OBEX).

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX server unregister.

detaileddescription.para: Unregister a registered OBEX server.
The server should be disconnected when calling the function.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX connect request.

detaileddescription.para: The connect operation initiates the connection and sets up the basic expectations of each side of the link. The connect request must fit in a single packet. The third parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the connect request is stored in thrid parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Such as header Authenticate Challenge is packed by calling bt_obex_add_header_auth_challenge. The OBEX object of the client should be set before calling the function. Before calling the API, bt_obex_client::ops should be initialized with valid address of type bt_obex_client_ops object. And the bt_obex_client::obex should be initialized with the valid OBEX object of type IrDA Oject Exchange Protocol (OBEX).

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_obex_server *server, uint8_t rsp_code, uint16_t mopl, struct net_buf *buf)
memberdef.name: bt_obex_connect_rsp

briefdescription.para: OBEX connect response.

detaileddescription.para: The connect response is used to acknowledged connect request packet. The connect response must fit in a single packet.

The second parameter rsp_code is used to pass the response code bt_obex_rsp_code. The typical values BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS for success. The 4th parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the connect response is stored in thrid parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Such as header Authenticate Response is packed by calling bt_obex_add_header_auth_rsp.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX disconnect request.

detaileddescription.para: The disconnect operation signals the end of the OBEX connection from client side. The disconnect request must fit in a single packet.

The second parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the disconnect request is stored in second parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Such as header Connection Id is packed by calling bt_obex_add_header_conn_id.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX disconnect response.

detaileddescription.para: The disconnect response is used to acknowledged disconnect request packet. The disconnect response must fit in a single packet.

The second parameter rsp_code is used to pass the response code bt_obex_rsp_code. The typical values, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS for success, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_UNAVAIL for service unavailable if the header Connection Id is invalid. The third parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the connect response is stored in third parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX put request.

detaileddescription.para: The put operation sends one object from the client to the server. The put operation consists of one or more request packets, the last of which should have the second parameter final set. The third parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the put request is stored in thrid parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Such as header Name is packed by calling bt_obex_add_header_name.

simplesect.para: A put operation with NO Body or End-of-Body headers whatsoever should be treated as a delete request. Similarly, a put operation with an empty End-of-Body header requests the recipient to create an empty object. This definition may not make sense or apply to every implementation (in other words devices are not required to support delete operations or empty objects).
Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX put response.

detaileddescription.para: The put response is used to acknowledged each put request packet. It is sent from the server to client. The second parameter rsp_code is used to pass the response code bt_obex_rsp_code. The typical values, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_CONTINUE for continue, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS for success. The third parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the put response is stored in thrid parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Such as header srm is packed by calling bt_obex_add_header_srm. Or, the buf could be NULL if there is not any data needs to be sent.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX get request.

detaileddescription.para: The get operation requests that the server return an object to the client. The get operation consists of one or more request packets, the last of which should have the second parameter final set, and the request phase of the get is complete. Once a get request is sent with the final bit, all subsequent get request packets must set the final bit until the operation is complete. The third parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the get request is stored in thrid parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Such as header Name is packed by calling bt_obex_add_header_name. Or, the buf could be NULL if there is not any data needs to be sent.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX get response.

detaileddescription.para: The get response is used to acknowledged get request packets. It is sent from the server to client. If the server has more than one object that fits the request, the behavior is system dependent. But the server device must not begin sending the object body chunks until the request phase is complete.

The second parameter rsp_code is used to pass the response code bt_obex_rsp_code. The typical values, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_CONTINUE for continue, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS for success. A successful response for an object that fits entirely in one response packet is BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS (Success, with Final bit set) in the response code, followed by the object body. If the response is large enough to require multiple GET responses, only the last response is BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS, and the others are all BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_CONTINUE (Continue). The object is returned as a sequence of headers just as with put operation. Any other response code bt_obex_rsp_code indicates failure. The typical failure response codes, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_BAD_REQ for Bad request - server couldn’t understand request, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_FORBIDDEN for Forbidden - operation is understood but refused. The third parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the get response is stored in thrid parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Such as header srm is packed by calling bt_obex_add_header_srm. Or, the buf could be NULL if there is not any data needs to be sent.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX abort request.

detaileddescription.para: The abort request is used when the client decides to terminate a multi-packet operation (such as put) before it would be normally end. The abort request always fits in one OBEX packet and have the Final bit set. The second parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the abort request is stored in thrid parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Such as header description is packed by calling bt_obex_add_header_description. Or, the buf could be NULL if there is not any data needs to be sent.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX abort response.

detaileddescription.para: The abort response is used to acknowledged abort request packet. It is sent from the server to client. The abort response always fits in one OBEX packet and have the Final bit set.

The second parameter rsp_code is used to pass the response code bt_obex_rsp_code. The typical value BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS for success. The third parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the abort response is stored in thrid parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Or, the buf could be NULL if there is not any data needs to be sent.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX setpath request.

detaileddescription.para: The setpath request is used to set the "current folder" on the receiving side in order to enable transfers that need additional path information. The Path name is contained in a Name header. The setpath request always fits in one OBEX packet and have the Final bit set.

The second parameter flags is the setpath operation flag bit maps. Bit 0 means backup a level before applying name (equivalent to ../ on many systems). Bit 1 means Don’t create folder if it does not exist, return an error instead. Other bits must be set to zero by sender and ignored by receiver. The third parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the setpath request is stored in thrid parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Such as header Name is packed by calling bt_obex_add_header_name. If the header Name is added, it means the client wants to go one level down relative to the current folder into the folder name. Or, the client wants to go back to the default folder when no bit of flags set.

simplesect.para: The Name header may be omitted when flags or constants indicate the entire operation being requested. For example, back up one level (bit 0 of flags is set), equivalent to "cd .." on some systems.
Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX setpath response.

detaileddescription.para: The setpath response is used to acknowledged setpath request packet. It is sent from the server to client. The setpath response always fits in one OBEX packet and have the Final bit set.

The second parameter rsp_code is used to pass the response code bt_obex_rsp_code. The typical value BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS for success. Any other response code bt_obex_rsp_code indicates failure. The typical failure response codes, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_BAD_REQ for Bad request - server couldn’t understand request, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_FORBIDDEN for Forbidden - operation is understood but refused. The third parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the setpath response is stored in thrid parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Or, the buf could be NULL if there is not any data needs to be sent.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX action request.

detaileddescription.para: The action operation is defined to cover the needs of common actions. The Action Id header BT_OBEX_HEADER_ID_ACTION_ID is used in the action operation and contains an action identifier which defines what action is to be performed. All actions are optional and depends on the implementation of server.
There are three actions defined bt_obex_action_id,

BT_OBEX_ACTION_COPY is used to copy an object from one location to another. The header Name BT_OBEX_HEADER_ID_NAME specifies the source file name and the header Dest Name BT_OBEX_HEADER_ID_DEST_NAME specifies the destination file name. These two headers are mandatory for this action.

BT_OBEX_ACTION_MOVE_RENAME is used to move an object from one location to another. It can also be used to rename an object. The header Name BT_OBEX_HEADER_ID_NAME specifies the source file name and the header Dest Name BT_OBEX_HEADER_ID_DEST_NAME specifies the destination file name. These two headers are mandatory for this action.

BT_OBEX_ACTION_SET_PERM is used to set the access permissions of an object or folder. The header Name BT_OBEX_HEADER_ID_NAME specifies the source file name and the header Permissions BT_OBEX_HEADER_ID_PERM specifies the new permissions for this object. These two headers are mandatory for this action.

The action operation consists of one or more request packets, the last of which should have the second parameter final set. The third parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the action request is stored in thrid parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Such as header Name is packed by calling bt_obex_add_header_name.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: OBEX action response.

detaileddescription.para: The action response is used to acknowledged action request packets. It is sent from the server to client. The second parameter rsp_code is used to pass the response code bt_obex_rsp_code. The typical value BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS for success.
There are three actions defined bt_obex_action_id. For each action request, there are failure response codes corresponding to it.

For action BT_OBEX_ACTION_COPY, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_NOT_FOUND - Source object or destination folder does not exist. BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_FORBIDDEN - Cannot modify the permissions of the destination object/folder, permission denied. BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_DB_FULL - Cannot create object in destination folder, out of memory BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_CONFLICT - Cannot modify the permissions, sharing violation, object or Set Object Permissions Action busy. BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_NOT_IMPL - Set Object Permissions Action not supported. BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_NOT_MODIFIED - Cannot create folder/file, destination folder/file already exits.

For action BT_OBEX_ACTION_MOVE_RENAME, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_NOT_FOUND - Source object or destination folder does not exist. BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_FORBIDDEN - Cannot modify the permissions of the destination object/folder, permission denied. BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_DB_FULL - Cannot create object in destination folder, out of memory BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_CONFLICT - Cannot modify the permissions, sharing violation, object or Set Object Permissions Action busy. BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_NOT_IMPL - Set Object Permissions Action not supported. BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_NOT_MODIFIED - Cannot create folder/file, destination folder/file already exits.

For action BT_OBEX_ACTION_SET_PERM, BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_NOT_FOUND - Source object or destination folder does not exist. BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_FORBIDDEN - Cannot modify the permissions of the destination object/folder, permission denied. BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_NOT_IMPL - Set Object Permissions Action not supported. BT_OBEX_RSP_CODE_CONFLICT - Cannot modify the permissions, sharing violation, object or Set Object Permissions Action busy.

The third parameter buf saves the packet data (sequence of headers) of the action response is stored in thrid parameter buf. All headers are packed by calling function bt_obex_add_header_*. Or, the buf could be NULL if there is not any data needs to be sent.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Sequence of headers to be sent out.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: number of objects (used by Connect)

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: name of the object (often a file name)

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: type of object - e.g.

detaileddescription.para: text, html, binary, manufacturer specific

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: the length of the object in bytes.

parameterdescription.para: The length of the object in bytes.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: date/time stamp – ISO 8601 version - preferred.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: date/time stamp – 4 byte version (for compatibility only)

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: text description of the object.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: name of service that operation is targeted to.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: an HTTP 1.x header.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: a chunk of the object body.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: the final chunk of the object body.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_buf *buf, uint16_t mopl, uint16_t len, const uint8_t *body, uint16_t *added_len)
memberdef.name: bt_obex_add_header_body_or_end_body

briefdescription.para: Add Header: a chunk (may be a final chunk) of the object body.

detaileddescription.para: The function is used to help to add body/end body for the upperlayer. When the tail room of the buffer is more than the passed body room, and the total length of buffer is not more than the mopl if the body has been added, the header end body will be added. Or, the header body will be added.

parameterdescription.para: The MOPL of the OBEX connection

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: identifies the OBEX application, used to tell if talking to a peer.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: an identifier used for OBEX connection multiplexing.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_buf *buf, size_t count, const struct bt_obex_tlv data[])
memberdef.name: bt_obex_add_header_app_param

briefdescription.para: Add Header: extended application request & response information.

parameterdescription.para: Number of bt_obex_tlv structures in data.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_buf *buf, size_t count, const struct bt_obex_tlv data[])
memberdef.name: bt_obex_add_header_auth_challenge

briefdescription.para: Add Header: authentication digest-challenge.

parameterdescription.para: Number of bt_obex_tlv structures in data.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_buf *buf, size_t count, const struct bt_obex_tlv data[])
memberdef.name: bt_obex_add_header_auth_rsp

briefdescription.para: Add Header: authentication digest-response.

parameterdescription.para: Number of bt_obex_tlv structures in data.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: indicates the creator of an object.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: uniquely identifies the network client (OBEX server).

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: OBEX Object class of object.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: parameters used in session commands/responses.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: sequence number used in each OBEX packet for reliability.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: specifies the action to be performed (used in ACTION operation).

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: the destination object name (used in certain ACTION operations).

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: 4-byte bit mask for setting permissions.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: 1-byte value to setup Single Response Mode (SRM).

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Add Header: Single Response Mode (SRM) Parameter.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Helper for parsing OBEX header.

detaileddescription.para: A helper for parsing the header structure for OBEX packets. The most common scenario is to call this helper on the in the callback of OBEX server and client.

simplesect.para: This helper function will consume buf when parsing. The user should make a copy if the original data is to be used afterwards.

parameterdescription.para: OBEX packet as given to the callback of OBEX server and client.
Callback function which will be called for each element that's found in the data The callback should return true to continue parsing, or false to stop parsing.
User data to be passed to the callback.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: number of objects (used by Connect)

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: name of the object (often a file name)

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: type of object - e.g.

detaileddescription.para: text, html, binary, manufacturer specific

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: the length of the object in bytes.

parameterdescription.para: The length of the object in bytes.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: date/time stamp – ISO 8601 version - preferred.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: date/time stamp – 4 byte version (for compatibility only)

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: text description of the object.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: name of service that operation is targeted to.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: an HTTP 1.x header.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: a chunk of the object body.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: the final chunk of the object body.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: identifies the OBEX application, used to tell if talking to a peer.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: an identifier used for OBEX connection multiplexing.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint16_t len, const uint8_t *data, bool(*func)(struct bt_obex_tlv *tlv, void *user_data), void *user_data)
memberdef.name: bt_obex_tlv_parse

briefdescription.para: Helper for parsing OBEX TLV triplet.

detaileddescription.para: A helper for parsing the TLV triplet structure for OBEX packets. The most common scenario is to call this helper on the in the callback of OBEX server and client. The data is encoded by using A Tag-Length-Value encoding scheme. Usually, it is the header value of the header Application Request-Response Parameters, Authenticate Challenge, and Authenticate Response. It means the data is the output of the bt_obex_get_header_app_param, bt_obex_get_header_auth_challenge, or bt_obex_get_header_auth_rsp.

parameterdescription.para: The data as given to the callback of OBEX server and client.
Callback function which will be called for each TLV triplet that's found from the data. The callback should return true to continue parsing, or false to stop parsing.
User data to be passed to the callback.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: extended application request & response information.

detaileddescription.para: The parameter can be parsed by calling bt_obex_tlv_parse.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: authentication digest-challenge.

detaileddescription.para: The options can be parsed by calling bt_obex_tlv_parse.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: authentication digest-response.

detaileddescription.para: The options can be parsed by calling bt_obex_tlv_parse.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: indicates the creator of an object.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: uniquely identifies the network client (OBEX server).

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: oBEX Object class of object.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: parameters used in session commands/responses.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: sequence number used in each OBEX packet for reliability.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: specifies the action to be performed (used in ACTION operation).

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: the destination object name (used in certain ACTION operations).

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: 4-byte bit mask for setting permissions.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: 1-byte value to setup Single Response Mode (SRM).

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Get header value: Single Response Mode (SRM) Parameter.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Make the UUID with specific data and len.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Check if the string is valid.

simplesect.para: true if the string is valid or false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check whether the buf has the specified header.

simplesect.para: true if the header is found or false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check whether the buf has the specified application parameter.

parameterdescription.para: The tag id of the application parameter.

simplesect.para: true if the tag is found or false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: The OBEX packet header length for PUT and GET request and response.
The max PDU data length of OBEX pachet.
The header length of the OBEX header body/end body.
The max remaining length of the buffer if the adding header is body/end body.
OBEX digest-challenge tag: Nonce String of bytes representing the nonce.
OBEX digest-challenge tag: Options Optional Challenge Information.
Option BIT0: When set, the User Id must be sent in the authenticate response.
Option BIT1: Access mode: Read Only when set, otherwise Full access is permitted.
OBEX digest-challenge tag: Realm A displayable string indicating which userid and/or password to use.

detaileddescription.para: The first byte of the string is the character set to use. The character set uses the same values as those defined in IrLMP for the nickname.

briefdescription.para: OBEX digest-Response tag: Request-digest String of bytes representing the request digest.
OBEX digest-Response tag: User Id User ID string of length n.

detaileddescription.para: Max size is 20 bytes.

briefdescription.para: OBEX digest-Response tag: Nonce The nonce sent in the digest challenge string.
IrDA Oject Exchange Protocol (OBEX)
